question: All the elements of oxygen family are
question_latex: All the elements of oxygen family are
solution: All elements of the oxygen family (Group 16) exhibit polymorphism.

question: Purest form of silica is:
question_latex: Purest form of silica is:
solution: Quartz is the purest form of silica.

question: On heating KClO3 we get
question_latex: On heating $KClO_3$ we get
solution: $2KClO_3 \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2KCl + 3O_2$

question: An alumina-silica clay, called bentonite is dropped from aeroplanes in the slurry form for:
question_latex: An alumina-silica clay, called bentonite is dropped from aeroplanes in the slurry form for:
solution: Bentonite is spread to destroy bacteria, insects and other pests by exposure to poisonous gas or smoke. This is called fumigation.

question: Which one of the following elements is most metallic?
question_latex: Which one of the following elements is most metallic?
solution: Metallic character increases down the group. Hence $Bi$ is most metallic.

question: White phosphorus is
question_latex: White phosphorus is
solution: White phosphorous is $P_4$ and has a tetrahedral structure.

question: Pure CO can be obtained from:
question_latex: Pure $CO$ can be obtained from:
solution: $Ni(CO)_4 \xrightarrow{\Delta} Ni + 4CO$

question: Which one will liberate Br2 from KBr?
question_latex: Which one will liberate $Br_2$ from $KBr$?
solution:
$2KBr + Cl_2 \rightarrow 2KCl + Br_2$
$Cl_2$ is a stronger oxidising agent than $Br_2$ and can displace $Br_2$ from $KBr$.

question: Boron was isolated by:
question_latex: Boron was isolated by:
solution: Davy isolated boron.

question: Consider the following reactions in which atoms have been labelled with isotopes (indicated by *): (I) S*SO3^2- + 2H+ → H2SO4 + S  (II) Na2HgI2I2* + 2AgNO3 → 2AgI + HgI2 + 2NaNO3. In which case isotopes are equivalent in the products?
question_latex:
Consider the following reactions in which atoms have been labelled with isotopes (indicated by *):
(I) $S^*SO_3^{2-} + 2H^+ \rightarrow H_2SO_4 + S$
(II) $Na_2HgI_2I_2^* + 2AgNO_3 \rightarrow 2AgI + HgI_2 + 2NaNO_3$
In which case isotopes are equivalent in the products?
solution: In reaction II, the labelled and unlabelled iodine atoms become equivalent in the products $AgI$ and $HgI_2$.

question: Dissociation of HI into H2 and I2 is
question_latex: Dissociation of $HI$ into $H_2$ and $I_2$ is
options: C. Kinetically as well as thermodynamically controlled
D. Neither kinetically nor thermodynamically controlled
solution: The dissociation of $HI$ into $H_2$ and $I_2$ is kinetically controlled.

question: Which of the following is the strongest reducing agent?
question_latex: Which of the following is the strongest reducing agent?
solution: $BiH_3$ is the strongest reducing agent while $NH_3$ is the weakest reducing agent in the group 15 hydrides.

question: The catalyst used in Friedel-Crafts reaction is:
question_latex: The catalyst used in Friedel-Crafts reaction is:
solution: Anhydrous $AlCl_3$ acts as a Lewis acid catalyst in Friedel-Crafts reaction.

question: In electrolysis of aluminium oxide which of the following is added to accelerate the process?
question_latex: In electrolysis of aluminium oxide which of the following is added to accelerate the process?
solution: Cryolite ($Na_3AlF_6$) is added to lower the melting point of alumina and to increase its electrical conductivity.

question: The colour of copper metaborate and chromium metaborate are respectively:
question_latex: The colour of copper metaborate and chromium metaborate are respectively:
solution: $Cu(BO_2)_2$ is blue and chromium metaborate is green.

question: The correct decreasing order of basic strength is:
question_latex: The correct decreasing order of basic strength is:
solution: As the size of the central atom increases, the lone pair of electrons occupies a larger volume. Electron density on the central atom decreases and consequently its tendency to donate electrons (basic character) decreases from $NH_3$ to $BiH_3$.

question: Which one of the following arrangements does not give the correct picture of the trends indicated against it? (i) F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2 : Oxidizing power (ii) F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2 : Electron gain enthalpy (iii) F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2 : Bond dissociation energy (iv) F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2 : Electronegativity
question_latex:
Which one of the following arrangements does not give the correct picture of the trends indicated against it?
(i) $F_2 > Cl_2 > Br_2 > I_2$ : Oxidizing power
(ii) $F_2 > Cl_2 > Br_2 > I_2$ : Electron gain enthalpy
(iii) $F_2 > Cl_2 > Br_2 > I_2$ : Bond dissociation energy
(iv) $F_2 > Cl_2 > Br_2 > I_2$ : Electronegativity
solution: The electron gain enthalpy of $F_2$ is not the highest (it is less than $Cl_2$ due to small size and high electron-electron repulsion). Also, bond dissociation energy of $F_2$ is less than $Cl_2$. So options (ii) and (iii) are incorrect trends.

question: Number of unpaired electrons in inert gas is
question_latex: Number of unpaired electrons in inert gas is
solution: Inert gases have completely filled orbitals and do not contain any unpaired electrons.

question: Which is a correct statement about diborane structure?
question_latex: Which is a correct statement about diborane ($B_2H_6$) structure?
options: A. All HBH bond angles are equal
B. All H-B bond lengths are equal
C. It has two three-centre-2 electron bonds
D. All hydrogen and boron atoms are in one plane
answer: C. It has two three-centre-2 electron bonds
solution: Diborane ($B_2H_6$) contains two three-centre two-electron (3c-2e) B-H-B bridge bonds, also known as banana bonds. The terminal and bridge H atoms are not equivalent, so bond lengths and bond angles are not all equal.

question: Among the oxyacids of phosphorus, the dibasic acid is
question_latex: Among the oxyacids of phosphorus, the dibasic acid is
solution:
In $H_3PO_3$ (phosphorous acid), there are two $P-OH$ bonds (ionisable $H^+$) and one $P-H$ bond (non-ionisable). Therefore it is dibasic.
$H_3PO_3 \rightleftharpoons 2H^+ + HPO_3^{2-}$

question: The order of acidic strength of boron trihalides
question_latex: The order of acidic strength of boron trihalides
solution: Boron trihalides are Lewis acids. The $p\pi - p\pi$ back bonding from halogen to boron is maximum in $BF_3$ (due to comparable size of B and F) and decreases down the group. This back bonding reduces Lewis acid character. Hence Lewis acidity order: $BF_3 < BCl_3 < BBr_3 < BI_3$.

question: The inert gas abundantly found in atmosphere is
question_latex: The inert gas abundantly found in atmosphere is
solution: Argon ($Ar$) is the most abundant noble gas in the atmosphere (0.93%), followed by $Ne$, $He$, $Kr$, and $Xe$.

question: In the reaction 4HNO3 + P4O10 → 4HPO3 + X, the product X is
question_latex: In the reaction $4HNO_3 + P_4O_{10} \rightarrow 4HPO_3 + X$, the product $X$ is
solution:
$4HNO_3 + P_4O_{10} \rightarrow 4HPO_3 + 2N_2O_5$
$P_4O_{10}$ is a strong dehydrating agent that removes water from $HNO_3$ to give $N_2O_5$.

question: Estimation of ozone can be made quantitatively by
question_latex: Estimation of ozone can be made quantitatively by
options: A. Decomposition into O2 and absorption of O2 into pyrogallol
B. Volumetric method using KI and titration of the liberated iodine using hypo solution
answer: B. Volumetric method using KI and titration of the liberated iodine using hypo solution
solution:
$KI + H_2O + O_3 \rightarrow I_2 + KOH + O_2$
$I_2 + 2Na_2S_2O_3 \rightarrow 2NaI + Na_2S_4O_6$
The liberated iodine is titrated against sodium thiosulphate (hypo) solution.

question: Which characteristic is not correct about H2SO4?
question_latex: Which characteristic is not correct about $H_2SO_4$?
solution: In $H_2SO_4$, the $S$ atom is present in its highest oxidation state of $+6$. Hence $H_2SO_4$ can act only as an oxidant (gains electrons), not as a reducing agent.

question: The gas not absorbed by coconut charcoal is
question_latex: The gas not absorbed by coconut charcoal is
solution: Helium ($He$) has the smallest size and lowest polarisability among noble gases, so it is not absorbed by coconut charcoal.

question: Which of the following noble gas is least polarisable?
question_latex: Which of the following noble gas is least polarisable?
solution: The smaller the size, the least is the polarisability. $He$ has the smallest atomic size among noble gases and hence is least polarisable.

question: What is Z in the following reactions: CuSO4 + Z → Cu3P2 + H2SO4 and HgCl2 + Z → Hg3P2 + HCl?
question_latex:
What is $Z$ in the following reactions:
$CuSO_4 + Z \rightarrow Cu_3P_2 + H_2SO_4$ and $HgCl_2 + Z \rightarrow Hg_3P_2 + HCl$?
solution:
$3CuSO_4 + 2PH_3 \rightarrow Cu_3P_2 + 3H_2SO_4$
$3HgCl_2 + 2PH_3 \rightarrow Hg_3P_2 + 6HCl$
$Z$ is Phosphine ($PH_3$).

question: When orthophosphoric acid is heated to 600°C, the product formed is
question_latex: When orthophosphoric acid is heated to $600^\circ C$, the product formed is
solution:
$2H_3PO_4 \xrightarrow{600^\circ C, -2H_2O} 2HPO_3$
On heating orthophosphoric acid to $600^\circ C$, metaphosphoric acid ($HPO_3$) is formed.

question: Which one is most stable to heat?
question_latex: Which one is most stable to heat?
solution: $HClO_4$ (perchloric acid) is the most thermally stable among the oxyacids of chlorine. Stability increases with increasing oxidation state of Cl: $HClO < HClO_2 < HClO_3 < HClO_4$.

question: Basicity of orthophosphoric acid is
question_latex: Basicity of orthophosphoric acid is
solution: Orthophosphoric acid ($H_3PO_4$) contains three $P-OH$ bonds and is therefore tribasic (basicity = 3).

question: Magnalium contains
question_latex: Magnalium contains
solution: Magnalium is an alloy of Aluminium ($Al$) and Magnesium ($Mg$).

question: Which of the following does not have a tetrahedral structure?
question_latex: Which of the following does not have a tetrahedral structure?
solution: $BH_3$ has $sp^2$-hybridized boron and is planar (trigonal), not tetrahedral. It exists as $B_2H_6$ (diborane). $NH_4^+$, $BH_4^-$, and $CH_4$ all have tetrahedral structures.

question: Which is not correct?
question_latex: Which is not correct?
options: B. GeCl2 is more stable than GeCl4
D. GeCl4 in HCl forms [GeCl6]2- ion
answer: B. GeCl2 is more stable than GeCl4
solution: Ge belongs to group 14 and possesses more tendency to show the $+4$ oxidation state. Therefore $GeCl_4$ is more stable than $GeCl_2$, making option B incorrect.

question: Which of the following order is/are incorrect regarding the property indicated against it? (i) HF > HI > HBr > HCl : Thermal stability (ii) Cl2O7 > Cl2O6 > ClO2 > Cl2O : Acidic character (iii) SbCl3 > SbCl5 : Covalent character (iv) MCl > MBr : Ionic character
question_latex:
Which of the following order is/are incorrect regarding the property indicated against it?
(i) $HF > HI > HBr > HCl$ : Thermal stability
(ii) $Cl_2O_7 > Cl_2O_6 > ClO_2 > Cl_2O$ : Acidic character
(iii) $SbCl_3 > SbCl_5$ : Covalent character
(iv) $MCl > MBr$ : Ionic character
solution: Metal halides with higher oxidation state are more covalent than those in a lower oxidation state. Hence $SbCl_5$ is more covalent than $SbCl_3$, making (iii) incorrect.

question: There are two H-bridge bonds in diborane molecule because there are:
question_latex: There are two H-bridge bonds in diborane molecule because there are:
options: C. 2 electrons less than required for bonding
D. Two electrons more than required for bonding
answer: C. 2 electrons less than required for bonding
solution: Diborane ($B_2H_6$) possesses four $B-H$ covalent bonds and two three-centred two-electron $B-H-B$ hydrogen bridge bonds (banana bonds). There are 2 electrons less than what is required for conventional bonding, hence the 3c-2e bridge bonds are formed.

question: Ammonia on reaction with hypochlorite anion can form:
question_latex: Ammonia on reaction with hypochlorite anion can form:
solution:
$N_2H_4$ and $NH_4Cl$ are both obtained by reaction of ammonia with hypochlorite anion:
$3NH_3 + NaOCl \rightarrow N_2H_4 + NH_4Cl + NaOH$

question: Which of the following is not correctly matched?
question_latex: Which of the following is not correctly matched?
solution: All hexafluorides of group 16 elements are gaseous in nature. $SF_6$ is a gas, not a solid.

question: Assertion: Bond angle of H2S is smaller than H2O. Reason: Electronegativity of the central atom increases, bond angle decreases.
question_latex:
Assertion: Bond angle of $H_2S$ is smaller than $H_2O$.
Reason: Electronegativity of the central atom increases, bond angle decreases.
(A) Assertion is correct, reason is correct; reason is a correct explanation for assertion.
(B) Assertion is correct, reason is correct; reason is not a correct explanation for assertion.
(C) Assertion is correct, reason is incorrect.
(D) Assertion is incorrect, reason is correct.
options: A. Assertion correct, reason correct; reason is correct explanation
B. Assertion correct, reason correct; reason is not correct explanation
solution:
Bond angle of $H_2S$ (92°) $< H_2O$ (104.5°). The assertion is correct.
However, the reason is incorrect: as electronegativity of the central atom *decreases*, bond angle decreases (bond pairs move away from central atom, reducing repulsion). $S$ is less electronegative than $O$, so bond pairs in $H_2S$ are farther from S, giving a smaller bond angle.

question: Which of the following statement is incorrect for group 15 elements?
question_latex: Which of the following statement is incorrect for group 15 elements?
options: A. Order of ionization enthalpies is ΔiH1 < ΔiH2 < ΔiH3
B. The boiling point and melting point increases from top to bottom in the group
C. Dinitrogen is a gas while all others are solids
answer: B. The boiling point and melting point increases from top to bottom in the group
solution: The melting point in group 15 increases up to arsenic ($As$) and then decreases up to bismuth ($Bi$). So statement B is incorrect.

question: Of the following acids I. Hypophosphorous acid II. Oxalic acid III. Glycine
question_latex:
Of the following acids:
I. Hypophosphorous acid
II. Oxalic acid
III. Glycine
options: A. I, II are monobasic, III dibasic and amphoteric
B. II monobasic, I dibasic acid, III amphoteric
C. I monobasic, II dibasic, III amphoteric
D. I, II dibasic, III amphoteric
answer: C. I monobasic, II dibasic, III amphoteric
solution:
Hypophosphorous acid ($H_3PO_2$) has only one $P-OH$ bond → monobasic.
$H_3PO_2 \rightleftharpoons H^+ + H_2PO_2^-$
Oxalic acid has two $-COOH$ groups → dibasic.
Glycine has both $-NH_2$ (basic) and $-COOH$ (acidic) groups → amphoteric.

question: H2S precipitates aqueous ZnCl2 solution in
question_latex: $H_2S$ precipitates aqueous $ZnCl_2$ solution in
solution:
$ZnCl_2$ solution is acidic due to hydrolysis: $ZnCl_2 + H_2O \rightarrow Zn(OH)_2 + 2HCl$
$ZnS$ is not precipitated in acidic medium, but in neutral medium. The $HCl$ formed is neutralized by $NH_4OH/NH_4Cl$ buffer.

question: The glass having smallest coefficient of thermal expansion is:
question_latex: The glass having smallest coefficient of thermal expansion is:
solution: Pyrex glass has the smallest coefficient of thermal expansion and is therefore used to prepare laboratory glass apparatus.

question: In XeF2, XeF4, XeF6 the number of lone pairs on Xe are respectively
question_latex: In $XeF_2$, $XeF_4$, $XeF_6$ the number of lone pairs on $Xe$ are respectively
solution:
$XeF_2$: $Xe$ has 3 lone pairs (linear structure, $sp^3d$ hybridization)
$XeF_4$: $Xe$ has 2 lone pairs (square planar, $sp^3d^2$ hybridization)
$XeF_6$: $Xe$ has 1 lone pair (distorted octahedral, $sp^3d^3$ hybridization)

question: Which of the following is correct about the reaction: 3NaClO → NaClO3 + 2NaCl?
question_latex: Which of the following is correct about the reaction: $3NaClO \xrightarrow{heat} NaClO_3 + 2NaCl$?
options: B. Oxidation number of Cl decreases as well as increases in this reaction
C. This reaction is used for the manufacture of halates
solution: In $NaClO$, $Cl$ is in $+1$ oxidation state. In $NaClO_3$, $Cl$ is in $+5$ state (increase), and in $NaCl$, $Cl$ is in $-1$ state (decrease). Since the same element is simultaneously oxidised and reduced, this is a disproportionation reaction. It is also used in the manufacture of halates ($NaClO_3$).

question: The most abundant gas in ordinary air among the following is:
question_latex: The most abundant gas in ordinary air among the following is:
solution:
The composition of dry air is: $N_2 = 78.08\%$; $O_2 = 20.95\%$; $Ar = 0.93\%$; $CO_2 = 0.03\%$; $Ne = 0.0018\%$; $He = 0.0005\%$; $Kr = 0.0001\%$; $Xe = 0.00001\%$.
Among the options, Argon is the most abundant.

question: Assertion: Both rhombic and monoclinic sulphur exist as S8 but oxygen exists as O2. Reason: Oxygen forms p-p multiple bond due to small size and small bond length but p-p bonding is not possible in sulphur.
question_latex:
Assertion: Both rhombic and monoclinic sulphur exist as $S_8$ but oxygen exists as $O_2$.
Reason: Oxygen forms $p\pi - p\pi$ multiple bond due to small size and small bond length but $p\pi - p\pi$ bonding is not possible in sulphur.
(A) Assertion correct, reason correct; reason is correct explanation.
(B) Assertion correct, reason correct; reason is not correct explanation.
(C) Assertion correct, reason incorrect.
(D) Assertion incorrect, reason correct.
options: A. Assertion correct, reason correct; reason is correct explanation
B. Assertion correct, reason correct; reason is not correct explanation
answer: A. Assertion correct, reason correct; reason is correct explanation
solution: Oxygen, due to its small size, can form $p\pi - p\pi$ multiple bonds and exists as $O_2$. Sulphur has larger orbitals and $p\pi - p\pi$ bonding is not effective, so it forms single-bonded ring structures like $S_8$. Both assertion and reason are correct and the reason correctly explains the assertion.

question: At room temperature, HCl is a gas while HF is a low boiling liquid. This is because
question_latex: At room temperature, $HCl$ is a gas while $HF$ is a low boiling liquid. This is because
solution: Due to the high electronegativity of fluorine, $HF$ molecules form strong intermolecular hydrogen bonds. This raises its boiling point and makes it a liquid at room temperature, unlike $HCl$ which is a gas.

question: CO is poisonous gas, antidote for CO poisoning is
question_latex: $CO$ is a poisonous gas, antidote for $CO$ poisoning is
solution: Antidote for $CO$ poisoning is carbogen. Carbogen is a mixture of 90% oxygen and 5-10% carbon dioxide. It stimulates breathing and helps eliminate $CO$ from the blood.

question: Observe the following statements regarding purification of bauxite: I. During Hall's process, silica is removed as Si (vapour). II. Bauxite ore contaminated with Fe2O3 is purified in Baeyer's process. III. During Serpeck's process, AlN is formed. The correct answer is
question_latex:
Observe the following statements regarding purification of bauxite:
I. During Hall's process, silica is removed as $Si$ (vapour).
II. Bauxite ore contaminated with $Fe_2O_3$ is purified in Baeyer's process.
III. During Serpeck's process, $AlN$ is formed.
The correct answer is
options: A. I, II and III are correct
B. Only I and II are correct
C. Only I and III are correct
D. Only II and III are correct
answer: D. Only II and III are correct
solution:
Red bauxite (containing $Fe_2O_3$ as main impurity) is refined by Baeyer's process or Hall's process (statement II correct). White bauxite (containing $SiO_2$ impurity) is refined by Serpeck's method, where $AlN$ is formed (statement III correct).
In Serpeck's method:
$Al_2O_3 \cdot 2H_2O + 3C + N_2 \xrightarrow{1800^\circ C} 2AlN + 3CO + 2H_2O$
$AlN + 3H_2O \rightarrow Al(OH)_3 + NH_3$
$2Al(OH)_3 \xrightarrow{\Delta} Al_2O_3 + 3H_2O$
Statement I is incorrect as silica is not removed as Si vapour in Hall's process.
